Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 
Error processing SSI file 
Expedited Partner Therapy 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
Expedited partner therapy (EPT) is a harm-reduction strategy and the clinical practice of treating the sex partners of persons with diagnosed chlamydia or gonorrhea, who are unable or unlikely to seek timely treatment, by providing medications or prescriptions to the patient as allowable by law. Patients then provide partners with these therapies without the health care provider having examined the partner (https:// www.cdc.gov/std/ept).
